stories: Good morning Sean, it's Thursday, the thirtieth of April, 2026
There are more trees on Earth than there are stars in our entire galaxy
Roughly three trillion trees against the Milky Way's couple hundred billion stars
And below the soil, those trees actually talk to one another through fungal networks the scientists call the wood wide web, sending chemical messages about drought, disease, and even insect attacks
So next time you walk past a forest, you're really walking past a quiet conversation
Saskatoon to Toronto is already behind you, the connection across to Seattle later this morning, and then the long haul back to Singapore in the evening
In between, you've got the spares risk follow-up at five thirty SGT, the PMO connect at six, and the Hypercare stabilisation forum at seven thirty Singapore time to round things off
The Marriott near Sea-Tac is the one fixed bed of the day before you're back in the air
Good morning Sean, it's Wednesday, the twenty-ninth of April, 2026
The next time you see a flash of lightning, remember you've just watched a column of air get heated to roughly thirty thousand kelvin
That's about five times hotter than the surface of the Sun, packed into a strike that lasts a fraction of a second
The sound that follows is just air expanding so violently it slams back into itself
You've got a morning run from seven, then it's wheels up
Travel to the airport from eight, the flight from Saskatoon to Toronto departs at ten thirty, and you connect on to Seattle at five thirty in the evening, landing just before one in the morning
The Marriott near Sea-Tac is your bed for the night
In between flights you've got a follow-up at three thirty on the spares risk for ramp-up and early ops
Good morning Sean, it's Tuesday, the twenty-eighth of April, 2026
Venus rotates so slowly that a single Venusian day stretches across about two hundred and forty-three Earth days, while the planet loops around the Sun in just two hundred and twenty-five
So on Venus, one day is genuinely longer than one year, and on top of that, it spins backwards, so the Sun rises in the west and sets in the east
A planet where time, in a real sense, runs sideways
Looking at your Saskatoon Tuesday, it's a cleaner one than yesterday
One quick bookend, then deep work for the rest of the day
And on a lighter note, Changi Airport and the National Arts Council are kicking off a new partnership from later this year to bring live arts performances and installations into the terminals
The world's nicest airport leaning even further into theatre
